div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church as well present as to come, Robert of Barnoldby sends greeting. Know me by the consent and will of my heirs to have given and granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, for the soul of my lord Ansketil of Ryedale and of all his ancestors and successors, and for my own soul and of all my ancestors’ and successors’ souls, twenty acres of land, namely ‘Raven’s Fen’ by the boundaries which I and the monks of Melrose walked around, namely, from the upper head of ‘Harrowdean’, the whole sedge-bed, upwards as far as the land that William of Ryedale gave to Matilda Corbet his wife as a marriage-portion, and from there upwards towards Whitton as the ditch is drawn between the said land and ‘Raven’s Fen’, as far as the trench which the monks made, and from there across towards Harrow, as far as the small thornbush, and from there as the the trenches have been made between the land of the lord Patrick and ‘Raven’s Fen’ as far as ‘Harecar’, and from there as the old fosse descends to ‘Harrowdean’. This whole land which is comprised between the said boundaries with all its pertinents I have given to the said church in return for twenty acres of land free, loosed and quit from every earthly service and custom and secular exaction, to be held and possessed as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably as it holds and possesses any alms. For I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the monks the said land against all men in respect of all things that can be exacted in return for land. And so that this my alms should be strong and established for ever, my lord Patrick of Ryedale at my request has licensed it and affirmed it by his charter. And since I do not have a seal, the lord Simon, archdeacon of Glasgow, and William son of John to apply their seals to the present charter. With these witnesses: the lord Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow; Arnold abbot of Rievaulx; Simon, archdeacon of Glasgow; Patrick of Ryedale and Walter his son; William son of John; Richard le Nain; Henry of Mow; Simon of Grubbit; Adam of Chatto; Adam of Whitton; Ralph of Ryedale.
